chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1505 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on MA-17115/2016 2. For orders on office objection 3. For katcha peshi. 4. For hearing of MA-12777/2016 29.11.2016. Mr. Aslam Baig Leghari, Advocate alongwith petitioners. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional A.G = Today, petitioners are present in person and petitioner No.1- Mst.Aail Khatoon has reiterated that she wants to live with her lawfully wedded husband, the petitioner No.2 (Arbab Ali). In compliance of the order passed on last date of hearing, the petitioner No.1-Mst.Aail Khatoon had met with her parents and according to her learned Counsel, the parties have settled their dispute. However, at this juncture he states that respondent No.2-SHO P.S Sakrand District Shaheed Benazirabad is continuously causing harassment to the petitioners. In the parawise comments filed by the police officials, it has been categorically undertaken that police will play its impartial role in accordance with law and will not cause any harassment to the petitioners. This petition is disposed of with the above observations and with the directions that the respondents are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioners and violation of this order shall be considered as a disobedience of the Court orders and appropriate proceedings will then be initiated against the delinquent party including the police officials. It is also directed that if the petitioners approach the concerned police officials for protection, the same should be provided to them, but strictly in accordance with law.

This petition is disposed of with the above observations and with the directions that the respondents are restrained from causing any harassment to the petitioners and violation of this order shall be considered as a disobedience of the Court orders and appropriate proceedings will then be initiated against the delinquent party including the police officials. It is also directed that if the petitioners approach the concerned police officials for protection, the same should be provided to them, but strictly in accordance with law. JUDGE Shahid
